title: Image manager with metadata and file storage
purpose: Manages image files with metadata tracking, including creation, listing, retrieval, and deletion operations.

steps.content: Ensure the base directory exists for storing images.
Read all files from the base directory.
For each file ending in .meta.json, parse its metadata.
IF parsing succeeds, store the metadata in the index map.
IF parsing fails due to corruption, skip that metadata file.
IF the directory doesn't exist, initialize an empty index.

methods.name: create
methods.normalized_params: (params: { name: string; buffer: Buffer; mimeType: string }): Promise<Image>

steps.content: Validate that the provided mime type is allowed.
IF the mime type is not allowed, throw an error.
Validate that the image buffer size is within the maximum limit.
IF the buffer size exceeds the limit, throw an error.
Sanitize the image name to create a base identifier.
Generate a unique identifier for the image by appending suffixes if needed.
Attempt to create the metadata file with exclusive access.
IF the metadata file already exists, increment the suffix and retry.
IF too many collisions occur, throw an error.
Store the metadata in the index map.
Write the binary image data to disk.
Return the image object with metadata and path.
Iterate through all entries in the index map.
Construct a list item for each metadata entry.
Sort the list items by uploaded time in descending order.
Return the sorted list of image items.
Look up the metadata for the specified image ID.
IF no metadata exists, return null.
Construct and return an image object from the metadata.

methods.name: getContent
methods.normalized_params: (id: string): Promise<{ buffer: Buffer; mimeType: string } | null>

steps.content: Look up the metadata for the specified image ID.
IF no metadata exists, return null.
Read the binary data from the file path stored in metadata.
IF reading fails, return null.
Return the binary buffer and mime type.
Look up the metadata for the specified image ID.
IF no metadata exists, throw an error.
Delete the binary file associated with the image.
IF deletion of the binary fails, tolerate the error.
Delete the metadata file associated with the image.
IF deletion of the metadata fails, tolerate the error.
Remove the image entry from the index map.
Check if the specified image ID exists in the index map.
Return true if the ID exists, false otherwise.
Return the number of entries in the index map.
Clear all entries from the index map.
Map the provided mime type to its corresponding file extension.
IF no mapping exists, return 'bin' as the default extension.
